title: A phospholipid-like charge-reversible lipid for PEG-free, stable, and low-inflammatory mRNA lipid nanoparticles.
snippet: The development of lipid nanoparticles (LNPs) facilitated optimal usage of mRNA drugs to achieve clinical success during the COVID-19 pandemic. Conventional LNPs rely on ionizable lipids for mRNA encapsulation and endosomal escape. However, the high hydrophobicity of ionizable lipids leads to aggregation of these LNPs under physiological conditions, requiri...
importanceSignals: priority topic: gene therapy, translational medicine
